invoice_no: Find label "Invoice No", "Invoice Number", "No.", "N", or "№" (Cyrillic numero) near the "Commercial Invoice" header. The value may be alphanumeric with letters, digits, and hyphens (e.g., "N 24636", "A012025000000055", "260101MR01", "019246"). Keep the exact string as in the document and put it in invoice_no.
invoice_date: Look for "Date"/"Invoice date". The date can be on one line or split into D / M / Y. If no explicit label is present, the date may stand under the "INVOICE" header in the format "City, DD MONTH YYYY" (e.g., "SANTIAGO, 04 SEPTEMBER 2019"). Assemble the full date and, if possible, normalize to YYYY-MM-DD. Add the date to invoice_date.
buyer: Identify the buyer by labels "Buyer", "Customer", "Consignee", "Bill to", "SOLD TO", "MESSRS", "THE BUYER AND THE ADDRESS", "BUYER/CONSIGNEE". Keep the full name and, if shown, the address in a single line without abbreviations. Add the text to buyer.
seller: Identify the seller by labels "Seller", "Sellers", "Supplier", "Shipper", "Exporter", "SELLER/SHIPPER". If no such label exists (common in Chinese invoices like Qingdao Spring Food, Time Seafood), take the seller name from the document header (the company name with logo and contact details at the top of the page). Keep the full legal name and, if shown, the address in a single line. Add the text to seller.
producer: If the invoice shows "Producer"/"Manufacturer"/"Factory"/"PRODUCER NAME"/"MANUFACTURER、PACKER、SHIPPER" (Chinese-style enumeration with 、 separator), extract the legal name. The producer name may be followed by a registration number (e.g., "REGISTER 10735") on the same line. If the field is absent and the seller is also the producer per the document header, leave empty. If multiple producers are listed, join with "; ". Add to producer.
incoterms: Extract delivery terms (Incoterms) - a three-letter code (e.g., EXW/FOB/CFR/CIF/DAP) and, if shown, the place. Labels: "Incoterms", "Delivery Terms", "DELIVERY TERMS", "Terms of Delivery", "TERMS OF DELIVERY" (may have suffix "(INCOTERMS 2010)"). Incoterms can also appear inside the line-item table column header (e.g., "Unit Price RMB/BAGS FOB QINGDAO") or in parentheses next to the price (e.g., "(FOB DALIAN, CHINA)"). Keep exactly as in the document and add to incoterms.
terms_of_payment: Find payment terms by labels "Terms of payment", "Payment", "PAYMENT TERMS", "PAYMENT CONDITIONS", "TERMS OF PAYMENT", or under a "TERMS & CONDITIONS" header where payment terms are a sub-item. Keep the full wording (e.g., "ADV. 20%, BALANCE C/COPY DOC.") and add to terms_of_payment.
bank_details: Extract the bank details block (bank, account/IBAN, SWIFT/BIC, beneficiary and address). Keep it as one multiline text without normalization and add to bank_details.
proforma_no: If the invoice references a proforma, find "Proforma Invoice No."/"Proforma No."/"PI/P.I."/"PI NO.". Extract only the proforma number, do not confuse with the order number ("Order", "P.Code"). Add the value to proforma_no.
container_no: Extract container number(s). Format is typically 4 letters + 7 digits, optionally with a suffix (e.g., "BMOU928899-1"). Container is usually present only for sea shipments (CFR/CIF) — for FOB/EXW invoices the container is normally absent because it is assigned later (at port). Do NOT confuse with "TRUCK NUMBER" / "Truck Plate" (separate field for road transport, format like "61 K 8214 / 61 AAL 959"). If the container is absent, leave empty. If multiple values, join with "; ". Add the string to container_no.
HS_code: Find HS code by labels "HS CODE", "HS Code", "HS", "Tariff code", "CUSTOMS CODE", or "[Product Name] CODE" (e.g., "FROZEN RAINBOW TROUT HG CODE"). Format may be: 6 digits with dot ("0304.81"), 6 digits without separators ("030314"), 10 digits without separators ("0303899007"), or 10 digits with spaces ("0303 14 2000"). The code can be inside a separate field/column OR embedded inside the goods description block (common in Chinese invoices). Keep the code format as in the document and add it to HS_code.
total_price: Find the document total ("Total", sometimes with Incoterms and currency, e.g., "Total CFR USD", "TOTAL: ... USD", "CNY 225,000.00", "RMB ¥ 990604.79"). Prefer the printed total; if it is missing, sum the line totals. Keep currency and separators as in the document. Add the string to total_price.
products: The "products" block is expandable: create product_1, product_2, ... for each line item in the invoice, keeping the document order. For each product, fill all nested fields. If some attributes are given in a shared block (e.g., one Latin species for multiple lines), duplicate them in the relevant product_i. In token_refs, include references to the source tokens/fragments used.
products[*].name_product: Take the commercial name from the column "Product", "Description", "Description of goods", "DESCRIPTION OF GOODS", or "DESCRIPTION OF THE GOODS". If the description is multi-line, join lines with spaces. Do not include the Latin name (put it in latin_name). Add the text to products[i].name_product.
products[*].latin_name: Extract the Latin (biological) name in the format "Genus species" (binomial nomenclature, two words, typically all uppercase). Common locations: in parentheses after the commercial name (e.g., "(SALMO SALAR)", "(LEPIDOCYBIUM FLAVOBRUNNEUM)") OR on a separate line below the table labeled "LATIN NAME: ..." (Chilean format). Not every parenthesized text is Latin — ignore Russian product names written in Latin letters (e.g., "(CHUKA SALAD)", "(IKURA)"), packaging descriptions (e.g., "(1KG/BAG * 10/CARTON, IVP)"), and marketing notes. Filter strictly by the binomial pattern. Keep the case as in the document and add the text to products[i].latin_name.
products[*].size_product: Extract the size/grade (e.g., "3-4 LB (1.3-1.8 KG)", "80/120", "0,8-1,2 KG", "6-10KG"). If both imperial and metric units are shown, keep both. Add the text to products[i].size_product.
products[*].unit_box: Specify the pricing/accounting unit. Possible units: "BAG/BAGS", "BOX/BOXES", "CARTON/CTN/CTNS", "CASE/CASES". Extract from the price column header in the table, of the form "[currency] Price/[unit_box]", "Unit Price/[unit_box]", "Price per [unit_box]", "[unit] Price [currency]/[unit_box]" (e.g., "Unit Price RMB/BAGS", "PRICE PER CARTON", "KG/BOX"). Add the value to products[i].unit_box.
products[*].packages: Extract the number of packages by labels "Cases", "Cartons", "Packages", "Boxes", "Bags", "CTNS", "Total BAGS", "QUANTITY OF CARTONS". The number may also appear inside parentheses after the total weight (e.g., "25,000KGS (2,500CTNS)" — extract "2,500"). Keep the number as in the document (including separators). Add to products[i].packages.
products[*].price_per_unit: Extract unit price (e.g., "Price per carton 169,50", "RMB¥569.692000/bag", "175,00 USD/UNIT"). Keep currency if shown and the number format as in the document (dot/comma). Add the value to products[i].price_per_unit.
products[*].total_price: Extract the line total and add it to products[i].total_price.
